channel.title: The New Beverly Cinema
channel.description: Schedule and program for The New Beverly Cinema (Quentin Tarantino's revival theater in Los Angeles).

item.title: 5 Card Stud / Rough Night in Jericho (IB Tech Print)
item.description:
A stranger is caught cheating in a game of five-card stud in a Rincon, Colorado saloon in 1880. Van Morgan (Dean Martin) unsuccessfully tries to save the gambler’s life as the other five players lynch the cheat. Meanwhile, a gold rush has brought a group of outsiders to Rincon, like gun-toting preacher Jonathan Rudd (Robert Mitchum) and Lily Langford (Inger Stevens), with her collection of beautiful lady “barbers.” When two townspeople are murdered, Van remembers that both victims were playing in the fatal card game. Tension mounts as the killer stalks the other players… (Paramount) Dean Martin lives to kill! George Peppard kills to live! And only one can survive the Rough Night in Jericho . In his first, and only, role as the villain, Martin is the corrupt sheriff of Jericho, the Wild West town he once helped to clean up. He’s ruthless and mean, controlling all businesses except for one, the stagecoach line run by a strong-willed widow (Jean Simmons). But when he pushes too far, the town must turn to a former US Marshall (Peppard) for help in this violent, tension-filled western from the creator of The Rifleman .
Cast: Director: Henry Hathaway | Writer: Screenplay by Marguerite Roberts from a novel by Ray Gaulden | Starring: Dean Martin, Robert Mitchum, Inger Stevens, Roddy McDowall, Katherine Justice, John Anderson, Ruth Springford, Yaphet Kotto, Denver Pyle | Year: 1968 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Arnold Laven | Writer: Screenplay by Sydney Boehm and Marvin H. Albert from the novel "The Man in Black" by Marvin H. Albert | Starring: Dean Martin, George Peppard, Jean Simmons, John McIntire, Slim Pickens | Year: 1967 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 104 minuti

Orari: Wed April 22 - 7:30 pm / 9:45 pm

item.title: The Canadian (Silent with Live Music Score)
item.description:
Featuring live musical accompaniment by Cliff Retallick. Director William Beaudine headed from Hollywood to the farmlands outside Calgary to helm one of his most enduring pictures. Thomas Meighan stars as a humble farmer who enters a marriage of convenience with an icy English woman (Mona Palma). Can the new couple survive the hardships of homesteading.. and each other? Because of his relentless schedule, Beaudine didn’t see the finished film until 1970, when he received a standing ovation at the Los Angeles County Museum of Art, a month before his death at the age of 78.
Cast: Director: William Beaudine | Starring: Thomas Meighan, Mona Palma, Wyndham Standing | Year: 1926 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 80 minuti

Orari: Mon April 13 - 7:30 pm

item.title: Night of the Lepus
item.description:
How many eyes does horror have? How many times will terror strike? A rancher upset over the booming local rabbit population enlists the help of scientists. But their cutting-edge experiments could have oversized complications when a test subject escapes. Stuart Whitman, Janet Leigh, Rory Calhoun and DeForest Kelley headline a horrific creature feature favorite.
Cast: Director: William F. Claxton | Writer: Screenplay by Don Holliday and Gene R. Kearney based on the novel "The Year of the Angry Rabbit" by Russell Braddon | Starring: Stuart Whitman, Janet Leigh, Rory Calhoun, DeForest Kelley, Paul Fix, Melanie Fullerton | Year: 1972 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 88 minuti

Orari: Sat April 04 - 11:59 pm

item.title: Reservoir Dogs
item.description:
Seven total strangers team up for the perfect crime. They don’t know each other’s name. But they’ve got each other’s color. Quentin Tarantino’s incendiary directorial debut ignited the filmmaker’s distinctive voice and united him with some of his key players. When a jewelry store robbery goes wrong, Reservoir Dogs jumps between the crime’s setup and its bloody aftermath as the men try to ferret out who might be the rat in their ranks. “A brash, brutal crime-caper film, Reservoir Dogs has enough raw energy for 10 motion pictures and more than enough rough stuff to traumatize the sensitive. But not only does Dogs have teeth, it has brains.” – Jay Boyar, Orlando Sentinel “It’s extremely well-acted, written with flair and directed by a 29-year-old first-timer, Quentin Tarantino, who always knows where to put the camera, when to cut to a flashback and how to draw the best work from his brilliant cast.” – John Hartl, Film.com
Cast: Director: Quentin Tarantino | Writer: Quentin Tarantino | Starring: Harvey Keitel, Tim Roth, Michael Madsen, Chris Penn, Steve Buscemi, Lawrence Tierney, Eddie Bunker, Quentin Tarantino, Randy Brooks, Kirk Baltz, Steven Wright | Year: 1992 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 99 minuti

Orari: Fri April 24 - 11:59 pm

item.title: Shadow of the Vampire
item.description:
An Unspeakable Horror. A Creative Genius. Captured For Eternity. During the filming of F.W. Murnau’s Nosferatu , the strange actor playing the vampiric lead seems particularly convincing. Willem Dafoe is unforgettable as the iconic Max Schreck, while John Malkovich shines as Murnau in a deviously devilish delight from director E. Elias Merhige and producer Nicolas Cage. Cary Elwes, Eddie Izzard, and Udo Kier co-star.
Cast: Director: E. Elias Merhige | Writer: Steven Katz | Starring: John Malkovich, Willem Dafoe, Udo Kier, Cary Elwes, Catherine McCormack, Eddie Izzard | Year: 2000 | Country: UK/Luxembourg/USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 92 minuti

Orari: Sat April 25 - 11:59 pm

item.title: Three Wise Girls / Men in Her Life
item.description:
They scoffed at love – laughed at marriage – lived for luxury alone! A small town girl heads to the big city, where she and her gal pals run into a series of professional and romantic troubles. Top-billed for the first time, Jean Harlow stars alongside Mae Clarke, Marie Prevost, Walter Byron, and Andy Devine in an entertaining pre-code film from Columbia Pictures and director William Beaudine. A formerly wealthy socialite finds herself abandoned by her new suitor when he discovers she’s now broke. While trying to pick up the pieces of her life, she meets a gruff retired bootlegger who offers to pay her debts if she can instruct him how to be a gentleman. But complications ensue in this Pygmalion tale when her previous flame returns. Made during director William Beaudine’s brief association with Columbia Pictures, Men in Her Life is a prime slice of pre-code entertainment.
Cast: Director: William Beaudine | Starring: Jean Harlow, Mae Clarke, Walter Byron, Marie Prevost, Andy Devine | Year: 1932 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: William Beaudine | Starring: Lois Moran, Charles Bickford, Victor Varconi, Donald Dillaway | Year: 1931 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 75 minuti

Orari: Sat April 18 - 7:00 pm / 8:40 pm

item.title: Raiders of the Lost Ark
item.description:
Producer George Lucas, screenwriter Lawrence Kasdan, and director Steven Spielberg gift us with one of the greatest adventures to ever grace the big screen! Follow archeology professor Indiana Jones (Harrison Ford) in a whip-smart, thrill-a-minute escapade that finds our hero facing evil Nazis, booby-trapped temples, and ex-flame Marion Ravenwood (Karen Allen) in a race to uncover the lost Ark of the Covenant. “ Raiders of the Lost Ark is an out-of-body experience, a movie of glorious imagination and breakneck speed that grabs you in the first shot, hurtles you through a series of incredible adventures, and deposits you back in reality two hours later – breathless, dizzy, wrung-out, and with a silly grin on your face. 4 Stars” – Roger Ebert
Cast: Director: Steven Spielberg | Writer: screenplay by Lawrence Kasdan, story by George Lucas and Philip Kaufman | Starring: Harrison Ford, Karen Allen, Paul Freeman, Ronald Lacey, John Ryhs-Davies, Denholm Elliott, Alfred Molina | Year: 1981 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 115 minuti

Orari: Sat April 25 - 2:00 pm

item.title: High Society (1955) / Pride of the Blue Grass (1954)
item.description:
What a ball! What a brawl! Leo Gorcey, Huntz Hall and the Bowery Boys take Park Avenoo laff by laff and give you the lowdown on High Society . In the 37 th film in the long running franchise, Sach (Hall) finds himself as the pawn in a criminal scheme to steal an inheritance from a rightful heir. Sach, Slip (Gorcey), and their Bowery buddies will have to save the day and set things straight. In the maddening roar of pounding hoofbeats… danger lashes the Kentucky turf… and a girl’s throbbing lips seek a man’s burning kisses! Can a young jockey guide his unlikely charge to win the big race? And can a handsome horseman decide between the attentions of a wealthy equestrienne and the owner of the underdog hoofer? Lloyd Bridges and Vera Miles topline a sweet-natured rarity from director William Beaudine. Never released on home video!
Cast: Director: William Beaudine | Writer: Screenplay by Bert Lawrence & Jerome S. Gottler, Story by Edward Bernds & Elwood Ullman | Starring: Leo Gorcey, Huntz Hall, Bernard Gorcey, Amanda Blake | Year: 1955 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: William Beaudine | Writer: Screenplay & Story by Harold Shumate | Starring: Lloyd Bridges, Vera Miles, Margaret Sheridan, Arthur Shields, Michael Chapin | Year: 1954 | Country: USA | Format: 16mm
Durata: 71 minuti

Orari: Sun April 26 - 6:30 pm / 8:00 pm

item.title: Heart to Heart (1928)(Silent with Live Music Score)
item.description:
Featuring live musical accompaniment by Cliff Retallick. Mary Astor stars in a big time comedy of a small time gal who turned against her heart to become Princess – and then turned right back again to real love. A rich widow of Italian royalty returns to the small US town where she grew up. But when she arrives on an earlier train than expected, she’s mistaken for a disreputable seamstress instead. While she plays along for fun, a former flame sees through the ruse in director William Beaudine’s silent comedy super rarity.
Cast: Director: William Beaudine | Starring: Mary Astor, Lloyd Hughes, Louise Fazenda, Lucien Littlefield, Thelma Todd | Year: 1928 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 70 minuti

Orari: Sat April 11 - 7:30 pm

item.title: The Crime of the Century / Her Bodyguard
item.description:
He couldn’t stop himself from committing The Crime of the Century ! A desperate man confesses to a murder and pleads for the police to lock him up. The only problem? The horrific crime hasn’t even happened yet. But when a body turns up, the cops and a clever reporter will have to put the pieces together. Jean Hersholt (for whom the Academy’s Humanitarian Award is named) and Wynn Gibson star in a playful pre-code hypnotist whodunnit. Playing the newspaperman, Stuart Erwin reteams with his Make Me a Star director William Beaudine. Plus, a 60-second, William Castle-like break at the climax for the audience to guess the killer! A pre-code comedy ultra-rarity from director William Beaudine! A beautiful Broadway star is given fine jewelry by her amorous sugar daddy, and the benefactor recruits a bodyguard to protect his investment. But romantic complications ensue when the new hire falls in love with his work.
Cast: Director: William Beaudine | Starring: Jean Hersholt, Wynne Gibson, Stuart Erwin, Frances Dee, Gordon Westcott | Year: 1933 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: William Beaudine | Starring: Edmund Lowe, Wynne Gibson, Edward Arnold, Alan Dinehart | Year: 1933 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 71 minuti

Orari: Wed April 15 - 7:30 pm / 9:15 pm

item.title: Last Tango in Paris
item.description:
Paul, a middle-aged American living in emotional exile, comes to Paris when his estranged wife commits suicide. Chancing to meet young Frenchwoman named Jeanne, Paul enters into a sadomasochistic, carnal relationship with her, indirectly attacking the hypocrisy all around him and his own feelings of guilt through his raw, outrageous sexual behaviour. (Park Circus)
Cast: Director: Bernardo Bertolucci | Starring: Marlon Brando, Maria Schneider, Maria Michi, Giovanna Galletti, Jean-Pierre Léaud, Massimo Girotti | Year: 1972 | Country: Italy/France | Format: 35mm
Durata: 129 minuti

Orari: Tue March 31 - 7:30 pm

item.title: Romancing the Stone
item.description:
She’s a girl from the big city. He’s a reckless soldier of fortune. For a fabulous treasure, they share an adventure no one could imagine… or survive. A mousy romance novelist must head to South America to trade a mysterious map to save her kidnapped sister. But she’ll have to team with a dashing American rogue, if she’s going to make it through the wild jungle. Kathleen Turner, Michael Douglas, and Danny DeVito star in the rip-roaring blockbuster from director Robert Zemeckis.
Cast: Director: Robert Zemeckis | Writer: Diane Thomas | Starring: Michael Douglas, Kathleen Turner, Danny DeVito, Alfonso Arau, Manuel Ojeda | Year: 1984 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 105 minuti

Orari: Sun April 19 - 2:00 pm

item.title: Hot Fuzz
item.description:
In a town where nothing much goes on, a whole lot is about to go down. When London cop Nicholas Angel (Simon Pegg) is shipped off to quiet Sandford, several mysterious deaths will turn up on his watch. Now, it’s up to him and his new partner, the daft Danny Butterman (Nick Frost), to crack the case in director Edgar Wright’s buddy comedy classic. “The best, surely the smartest, English-language movie of the year to date.” – Richard Corliss, TIME Magazine “Everything an action-comedy should be. It achieves through parody what most films in the genre can’t accomplish straight.” – Nathan Rabin, The A.V. Club “Wright and Pegg are storytellers who weave their naughty bits into genuine characters and a plot. It’s a ridiculous plot, but one that’s absolutely in the spirit of the films they’re satirizing.” – Kevin Crust, Los Angeles Times
Cast: Director: Edgar Wright | Writer: Edgar Wright & Simon Pegg | Starring: Simon Pegg, Nick Frost, Jim Broadbent, Paddy Considine, Timothy Dalton, Anne Reid, Rafe Spall, Billie Whitelaw, Edward Woodward, Bill Nighy | Year: 2007 | Country: UK | Format: 35mm
Durata: 121 minuti

Orari: Sat April 18 - 11:59 pm

item.title: 5 Card Stud / Rough Night in Jericho (IB Tech Print)
item.description:
A stranger is caught cheating in a game of five-card stud in a Rincon, Colorado saloon in 1880. Van Morgan (Dean Martin) unsuccessfully tries to save the gambler’s life as the other five players lynch the cheat. Meanwhile, a gold rush has brought a group of outsiders to Rincon, like gun-toting preacher Jonathan Rudd (Robert Mitchum) and Lily Langford (Inger Stevens), with her collection of beautiful lady “barbers.” When two townspeople are murdered, Van remembers that both victims were playing in the fatal card game. Tension mounts as the killer stalks the other players… (Paramount) Dean Martin lives to kill! George Peppard kills to live! And only one can survive the Rough Night in Jericho . In his first, and only, role as the villain, Martin is the corrupt sheriff of Jericho, the Wild West town he once helped to clean up. He’s ruthless and mean, controlling all businesses except for one, the stagecoach line run by a strong-willed widow (Jean Simmons). But when he pushes too far, the town must turn to a former US Marshall (Peppard) for help in this violent, tension-filled western from the creator of The Rifleman .
Cast: Director: Henry Hathaway | Writer: Screenplay by Marguerite Roberts from a novel by Ray Gaulden | Starring: Dean Martin, Robert Mitchum, Inger Stevens, Roddy McDowall, Katherine Justice, John Anderson, Ruth Springford, Yaphet Kotto, Denver Pyle | Year: 1968 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Arnold Laven | Writer: Screenplay by Sydney Boehm and Marvin H. Albert from the novel "The Man in Black" by Marvin H. Albert | Starring: Dean Martin, George Peppard, Jean Simmons, John McIntire, Slim Pickens | Year: 1967 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 104 minuti

Orari: Tue April 21 - 7:30 pm / 9:45 pm

item.title: The Crime of the Century / Her Bodyguard
item.description:
He couldn’t stop himself from committing The Crime of the Century ! A desperate man confesses to a murder and pleads for the police to lock him up. The only problem? The horrific crime hasn’t even happened yet. But when a body turns up, the cops and a clever reporter will have to put the pieces together. Jean Hersholt (for whom the Academy’s Humanitarian Award is named) and Wynn Gibson star in a playful pre-code hypnotist whodunnit. Playing the newspaperman, Stuart Erwin reteams with his Make Me a Star director William Beaudine. Plus, a 60-second, William Castle-like break at the climax for the audience to guess the killer! A pre-code comedy ultra-rarity from director William Beaudine! A beautiful Broadway star is given fine jewelry by her amorous sugar daddy, and the benefactor recruits a bodyguard to protect his investment. But romantic complications ensue when the new hire falls in love with his work.
Cast: Director: William Beaudine | Starring: Jean Hersholt, Wynne Gibson, Stuart Erwin, Frances Dee, Gordon Westcott | Year: 1933 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: William Beaudine | Starring: Edmund Lowe, Wynne Gibson, Edward Arnold, Alan Dinehart | Year: 1933 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 71 minuti

Orari: Tue April 14 - 7:30 pm / 9:15 pm

item.title: Raiders of the Lost Ark
item.description:
Producer George Lucas, screenwriter Lawrence Kasdan, and director Steven Spielberg gift us with one of the greatest adventures to ever grace the big screen! Follow archeology professor Indiana Jones (Harrison Ford) in a whip-smart, thrill-a-minute escapade that finds our hero facing evil Nazis, booby-trapped temples, and ex-flame Marion Ravenwood (Karen Allen) in a race to uncover the lost Ark of the Covenant. “ Raiders of the Lost Ark is an out-of-body experience, a movie of glorious imagination and breakneck speed that grabs you in the first shot, hurtles you through a series of incredible adventures, and deposits you back in reality two hours later – breathless, dizzy, wrung-out, and with a silly grin on your face. 4 Stars” – Roger Ebert
Cast: Director: Steven Spielberg | Writer: screenplay by Lawrence Kasdan, story by George Lucas and Philip Kaufman | Starring: Harrison Ford, Karen Allen, Paul Freeman, Ronald Lacey, John Ryhs-Davies, Denholm Elliott, Alfred Molina | Year: 1981 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 115 minuti

Orari: Sun April 26 - 2:00 pm

item.title: The Exile / The Prisoner of Zenda (1937)
item.description:
King of Adventure! Queen of Romance! The rightful heir to the English throne is secretly living in exile in Holland due to unrest at home. As romance blossoms with a Dutch beauty, assassins arrive to kill the would-be king. Douglas Fairbanks, Jr., Rita Corday, and Maria Montez star in a swashbuckling romantic adventure stylishly directed by Max Ophüls. He has the appearance and manner of the king, yet he’s really a lookalike… and on his shoulders rests all hope of foiling a blackguard’s plot to usurp the throne. Adventure, pageantry and royal intrigue are forged at sword point in this big screen version of the beloved 1894 novel. Ronald Colman (A Tale of Two Cities) plays the double role in the resilient 1937 David O. Selznick production, making palpable the heartbreak of the royal stand-in whose gallantry is tested by his love for the real king’s fiancée (Madeleine Carroll). (Warner Bros)
Cast: Director: Max Ophüls | Writer: Written by Douglas Fairbanks Jr. based on the novel "His Majesty, The King" by Cosmo Hamilton | Starring: Douglas Fairbanks Jr., Maria Montez, Rita Corday [as Paule Croset], Henry Daniell, Nigel Bruce | Year: 1947 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: John Cromwell | Writer: Screen play by John L. Balderston based on Edward Rose's dramatization of Anthony Hope's novel | Starring: Ronald Colman, Madeleine Carroll, Douglas Fairbanks Jr., Mary Astor, C. Aubrey Smith, David Niven, Raymond Massey | Year: 1937 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 101 minuti

Orari: Wed April 01 - 7:30 pm / 9:35 pm

item.title: Make Me a Star / Don’t Gamble with Strangers
item.description:
Stars in their eyes – laughter in the aisles! Famed funnyman Stuart Erwin portrays smalltown hick and Hollywood hopeful Merton Gill in the comedic tale that was first a novel, a beloved (and periodically reprised) stage hit, and silent film. Erwin adds a wistful note to Merton’s Tinseltown dream in 1932’s Make Me a Star , including star cameos including then-current luminaries Tallulah Bankhead, Maurice Chevalier, Claudette Colbert, and Gary Cooper. (Warner Bros) When Mike Sarno (Kane Richmond) out-cheats Fay Benton (Bernadene Hayes) in a crooked poker game, the two gamblers bury the hatchet and agree to become partners in crime. Posing as brother and sister, the pair finally hit the big time when Mike runs into a gambler (Phil Van Zandt) who owes him a debt and takes over his casino to square it. As the high-rollers’ dough starts pouring in, Mike eventually tires of Fay and dumps her to get his hands on a gorgeous heiress (Gloria Warren) and her cash, a double-cross that will lead to murder and a shocking surprise when the cops reveal the killer’s name. (Warner Bros)
Cast: Director: William Beaudine | Writer: Screenplay by Sam Mintz & Walter Deleon and Arthur Kober based on the play “Merton of the Movies” by George S. Kaufman and Marc Connelly and the novel by Harry Leon Wilson | Starring: Joan Blondell, Stuart Erwin, Zasu Pitts, Ben Turpin | Year: 1932 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: William Beaudine | Starring: Kane Richmond, Bernadene Hayes, Peter Cookson, Gloria Warren | Year: 1946 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 67 minuti

Orari: Wed April 29 - 7:30 pm / 9:25 pm

item.title: Kill Bill: The Whole Bloody Affair
item.description:
Quentin Tarantino’s Kill Bill: The Whole Bloody Affair unites Volume 1 and Volume 2 into a single unrated epic – presented exactly as he intended, complete with a new, never-before-seen anime sequence. Uma Thurman stars as The Bride, left for dead after her former boss and lover Bill ambushes her wedding rehearsal, shooting her in the head and stealing her unborn child. To exact her vengeance, she must first hunt down the four remaining members of the Deadly Viper Assassination Squad before confronting Bill himself. With its operatic scope, relentless action, and iconic style, The Whole Bloody Affair stands as one of cinema’s definitive revenge sagas – rarely shown in its complete form and now presented with a classic intermission.
Cast: Director: Quentin Tarantino | Writer: Quentin Tarantino | Starring: Uma Thurman, Lucy Liu, Vivica A. Fox, Michael Madsen, Daryl Hannah, Gordon Liu, Michael Parks, David Carradine | Year: 2025 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 275 minuti

Orari: Sun April 05 - 6:30 pm

item.title: Training Day / Narc
item.description:
Denzel Washington delivers an Academy Award-winning performance opposite Ethan Hawke in this gritty drama set in the morally ambiguous world of undercover police work. Every day a war rages between drug dealers and cops on the streets of America’s inner cities. With every war come casualties, none greater than 13-year veteran Los Angeles narcotics officer Alonzo Harris (Washington), whose questionable methods blur the line between legal and corrupt. Today Alonzo gets a new partner, idealistic rookie Jake Hoyt (Hawke), and Jake has one day – and one day only – to prove his mettle to his fiercely charismatic superior. Over 24 hours, Jake will be dragged into the ethical mire of Alonzo’s logic as both men risk their careers and their lives to serve conflicting notions of justice. (Warner Bros) Jason Patric and Ray Liotta star in this sizzling action-drama that Elvis Mitchell at The New York Times says ”…has the velocity of a hot slug from the barrel of a gun.” Patric stars as Nick Tellis, a suspended narcotics officer recruited to investigate the murder of Michael Calvess, a rookie cop killed under mysterious circumstances. Tellis is teamed with Valvess’ partner, Henry Oak (Liotta), a hot-tempered renegade who’ll stop at nothing to avenge his friend’s death. As Tellis and Oak follow a shadowy trail through the sunny drug underworld, the lines start to blur – between right and wrong, good and evil, and justice and revenge. (Paramount)
Cast: Director: Antoine Fuqua | Writer: David Ayer | Starring: Denzel Washington, Ethan Hawke, Scott Glenn, Cliff Curtis, Dr. Dre, Snoop Dogg | Year: 2001 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Joe Carnahan | Writer: Joe Carnahan | Starring: Jason Patric, Ray Liotta, Busta Rhymes, Chi McBride | Year: 2002 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 105 minuti

Orari: Fri April 24 - 6:00 pm / 8:30 pm

item.title: Animal Crackers
item.description:
In perhaps their most frequently quoted film, Animal Crackers , the Marx Brothers transform their smash-hit Broadway musical to the big screen with uproarious results. An aristocratic house party in honor of famed explorer Captain Spaulding (Groucho) may have met its match when the Brothers unleash their trademark brand of wordplay, musical comedy and slapstick. “Groucho shines; Harpo remains a pantomimic clown who ranks with the highest; Chico adds an unusual comedy sense to his dialog as well as business and piano playing; and Zeppo, if in on a split, is lucky.” – Variety
Cast: Director: Victor Heerman | Starring: Groucho Marx, Harpo Marx, Chico Marx, Zeppo Marx, Lillian Roth, Margaret Dumont | Year: 1930 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 98 minuti

Orari: Sat April 11 - 2:00 pm

item.title: Penrod and Sam (1923)(Silent with Live Music Score)(Brand New 35mm Print)
item.description:
Featuring live musical accompaniment by Cliff Retallick. A nostalgic, heartfelt series of boyhood vignettes adapted from the blockbuster writing of Booth Tarkington, Penrod and Sam follows the titular boys and their neighborhood pals as they play, get into mischief, and embark on a series of childhood adventures. Director William Beaudine shows a deft hand at working with children (and a cute dog!), a gift that he would return to many times over his career.
Cast: Director: William Beaudine | Starring: Ben Alexander, Joe Butterworth, Buddy Messinger | Year: 1923 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 84 minuti

Orari: Sun April 19 - 6:30 pm

item.title: Detective Kitty O’Day / Bowery Champs
item.description:
A slew of laughs with a glamorous sleuth on a manhunt! When her boss is murdered, a fast-talking secretary Kitty O’Day will put her sleuthing skills to the test. But when bodies start to pile up, all fingers point to her as the killer. Jean Parker stars in a fun-filled mystery directed by William Beaudine, with co-screenwriter Tim Ryan memorably appearing as the exasperated police detective. It’s a case of moider and you’re the victim! You’ll die laffing as the Sidewalk Sockers blast the Tenement Terror! Muggs (Leo Gorcey) is working as a newspaper copy boy and he’s got the scoop on a big story. Now he’ll enlist Glimpy (Huntz Hall) and his East Side Kids gang to help investigate the murder of a nightclub owner. While the cops hunt for the dead man’s wife as their prime suspect, our boys have other ideas.
Cast: Director: William Beaudine | Writer: Screenplay by Tim Ryan and Victor Hammond, Original story by Victor Hammond | Starring: Jean Parker, Peter Cookson, Tim Ryan, Veda Ann Borg, Ed Gargan | Year: 1944 | Country: USA | Format: 16mm | Director: William Beaudine | Writer: Original Screenplay by Earle Snell | Starring: Leo Gorcey, Huntz Hall, Bill Benedict, Bobby Jordan, Gabriel Dell, Thelma White | Year: 1944 | Country: USA | Format: 16mm
Durata: 62 minuti

Orari: Mon April 20 - 7:30 pm / 9:00 pm

item.title: Reservoir Dogs
item.description:
Seven total strangers team up for the perfect crime. They don’t know each other’s name. But they’ve got each other’s color. Quentin Tarantino’s incendiary directorial debut ignited the filmmaker’s distinctive voice and united him with some of his key players. When a jewelry store robbery goes wrong, Reservoir Dogs jumps between the crime’s setup and its bloody aftermath as the men try to ferret out who might be the rat in their ranks. “A brash, brutal crime-caper film, Reservoir Dogs has enough raw energy for 10 motion pictures and more than enough rough stuff to traumatize the sensitive. But not only does Dogs have teeth, it has brains.” – Jay Boyar, Orlando Sentinel “It’s extremely well-acted, written with flair and directed by a 29-year-old first-timer, Quentin Tarantino, who always knows where to put the camera, when to cut to a flashback and how to draw the best work from his brilliant cast.” – John Hartl, Film.com
Cast: Director: Quentin Tarantino | Writer: Quentin Tarantino | Starring: Harvey Keitel, Tim Roth, Michael Madsen, Chris Penn, Steve Buscemi, Lawrence Tierney, Eddie Bunker, Quentin Tarantino, Randy Brooks, Kirk Baltz, Steven Wright | Year: 1992 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 99 minuti

Orari: Fri April 10 - 11:59 pm

item.title: Cartoon Club
item.description:
Calling all animation aficionados! Join us for a celebration of the art and history of one of our favorite mediums with our monthly Cartoon Club! Enjoy a feature-length assemblage of classic shorts, including rare gems unearthed from the vaults and other animated antics lovingly assembled from our large archive of film treasures.

Orari: Sat April 11 - 10:00 am

item.title: Make Me a Star / Don’t Gamble with Strangers
item.description:
Stars in their eyes – laughter in the aisles! Famed funnyman Stuart Erwin portrays smalltown hick and Hollywood hopeful Merton Gill in the comedic tale that was first a novel, a beloved (and periodically reprised) stage hit, and silent film. Erwin adds a wistful note to Merton’s Tinseltown dream in 1932’s Make Me a Star , including star cameos including then-current luminaries Tallulah Bankhead, Maurice Chevalier, Claudette Colbert, and Gary Cooper. (Warner Bros) When Mike Sarno (Kane Richmond) out-cheats Fay Benton (Bernadene Hayes) in a crooked poker game, the two gamblers bury the hatchet and agree to become partners in crime. Posing as brother and sister, the pair finally hit the big time when Mike runs into a gambler (Phil Van Zandt) who owes him a debt and takes over his casino to square it. As the high-rollers’ dough starts pouring in, Mike eventually tires of Fay and dumps her to get his hands on a gorgeous heiress (Gloria Warren) and her cash, a double-cross that will lead to murder and a shocking surprise when the cops reveal the killer’s name. (Warner Bros)
Cast: Director: William Beaudine | Writer: Screenplay by Sam Mintz & Walter Deleon and Arthur Kober based on the play “Merton of the Movies” by George S. Kaufman and Marc Connelly and the novel by Harry Leon Wilson | Starring: Joan Blondell, Stuart Erwin, Zasu Pitts, Ben Turpin | Year: 1932 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: William Beaudine | Starring: Kane Richmond, Bernadene Hayes, Peter Cookson, Gloria Warren | Year: 1946 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 67 minuti

Orari: Thu April 30 - 7:30 pm / 9:25 pm

item.title: Kill Bill: The Whole Bloody Affair
item.description:
Quentin Tarantino’s Kill Bill: The Whole Bloody Affair unites Volume 1 and Volume 2 into a single unrated epic – presented exactly as he intended, complete with a new, never-before-seen anime sequence. Uma Thurman stars as The Bride, left for dead after her former boss and lover Bill ambushes her wedding rehearsal, shooting her in the head and stealing her unborn child. To exact her vengeance, she must first hunt down the four remaining members of the Deadly Viper Assassination Squad before confronting Bill himself. With its operatic scope, relentless action, and iconic style, The Whole Bloody Affair stands as one of cinema’s definitive revenge sagas – rarely shown in its complete form and now presented with a classic intermission.
Cast: Director: Quentin Tarantino | Writer: Quentin Tarantino | Starring: Uma Thurman, Lucy Liu, Vivica A. Fox, Michael Madsen, Daryl Hannah, Gordon Liu, Michael Parks, David Carradine | Year: 2025 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 275 minuti

Orari: Sat April 04 - 5:30 pm

item.title: The Ape Man / Voodoo Man / Bela Lugosi Meets A Brooklyn Gorilla
item.description:
No one is safe from the cruel desires of this inhuman fiend! It’s WEIRD! It’s WILD! It’s a tale of TERROR you won’t forget! An experiment turns a scientist into a crazed half-human, half-ape killer and now he’ll have to scramble to find a cure. Bela Lugosi stars in a horror fave from producer Sam Katsman and director William Beaudine. You dare not look into his eyes! His lust for voodooism spells D-O-O-M! A madman lures women to his mansion with plans to use them to revive his dead wife. Bela Lugosi, John Carradine, and George Zucco star in a classic low budget chiller from producer Sam Katzman and director William Beaudine. Crooner (and future Massacre Mafia Style / Gone with the Pope filmmaker) Duke Mitchell and rubber-faced comedian Sam Patrello take their Martin & Lewis-style nightclub act to the big screen in a horror film that will stiffen you with laughter! When the pair land on the exotic island of Cola Cola, they’ll entertain the locals (while Duke woos the chief’s daughter) and try to find a way home. Will the mysterious scientist Dr. Zabor (Bela Lugosi) be able to help? William Beaudine directs from a script by his frequent Bowery Boys collaborator Tim Ryan.
Cast: Director: William Beaudine | Writer: Screenplay by Barney Sarecky, Original story by Karl Brown | Starring: Bela Lugosi, Louise Currie, Wallace Ford, Henry Hall | Year: 1943 | Country: USA | Format: 16mm | Director: William Beaudine | Writer: Original Story & Screenplay by Robert Charles | Starring: Bela Lugosi, John Carradine, George Zucco, Wanda McKay, Louise Currie | Year: 1944 | Country: USA | Format: 16mm | Director: William Beaudine | Writer: Screen play by Tim Ryan | Starring: Bela Lugosi, Duke Mitchell, Sammy Petrillo, Charlita, Muriel Landers, Al Kikume | Year: 1952 | Country: USA | Format: 16mm
Durata: 74 minuti

Orari: Tue April 28 - 7:30 pm / 9:05 pm / 10:25 pm

item.title: The Old Fashioned Way / Misbehaving Ladies
item.description:
W.C. Fields battles creditors, widows and obnoxious children in the comedy classic The Old Fashioned Way . Staying one step ahead of the law, The Great McGonigle (Fields) and his impoverished theatrical troupe arrive in a small town to perform The Drunkard, a Victorian temperance play. Discovering they have only sold a handful of tickets, McGonigle promises a part to the wealthy mother of an annoying toddler (Baby LeRoy) with her appearance guaranteeing a sell-out. Staving off the local sheriff who intends to collect the receipts, McGonigle gives a performance the town will never forget in an uproarious farce featuring Fields’ legendary juggling act! (Universal Studios) Reigning as a Royal Princess or disguised as a hometown seamstress, she kept wives guessing, husbands wandering, and tongues wagging! An American woman who married a European prince returns to the States and encounters an unexpected problem: people think she’s only the princess’ dressmaker. When she’s only recognized by her ex-boyfriend, they keep the charade going to have some fun, but a series of misunderstandings could cause trouble. Lila Lee and Ben Lyon star in a pre-code rarity that’s been nearly impossible to see for more than 90 years.
Cast: Director: William Beaudine | Writer: Screenplay by Garnet Weston & Jack Cunningham from a story by Charles Bogle [W.C. Fields] | Starring: W.C. Fields, Joe Morrison, Baby LeRoy, Judith Allen | Year: 1934 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: William Beaudine | Writer: Screenplay by Julien Josephson from the story 'Queen of Main Street" by Juliet Wilbur Tompkins | Starring: Lila Lee, Ben Lyon, Louise Fazenda, Lucien Littlefield | Year: 1931 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 75 minuti

Orari: Thu April 09 - 7:30 pm / 9:10 pm

item.title: Romancing the Stone
item.description:
She’s a girl from the big city. He’s a reckless soldier of fortune. For a fabulous treasure, they share an adventure no one could imagine… or survive. A mousy romance novelist must head to South America to trade a mysterious map to save her kidnapped sister. But she’ll have to team with a dashing American rogue, if she’s going to make it through the wild jungle. Kathleen Turner, Michael Douglas, and Danny DeVito star in the rip-roaring blockbuster from director Robert Zemeckis.
Cast: Director: Robert Zemeckis | Writer: Diane Thomas | Starring: Michael Douglas, Kathleen Turner, Danny DeVito, Alfonso Arau, Manuel Ojeda | Year: 1984 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 105 minuti

Orari: Sat April 18 - 2:00 pm

item.title: The Exile / The Prisoner of Zenda (1937)
item.description:
King of Adventure! Queen of Romance! The rightful heir to the English throne is secretly living in exile in Holland due to unrest at home. As romance blossoms with a Dutch beauty, assassins arrive to kill the would-be king. Douglas Fairbanks, Jr., Rita Corday, and Maria Montez star in a swashbuckling romantic adventure stylishly directed by Max Ophüls. He has the appearance and manner of the king, yet he’s really a lookalike… and on his shoulders rests all hope of foiling a blackguard’s plot to usurp the throne. Adventure, pageantry and royal intrigue are forged at sword point in this big screen version of the beloved 1894 novel. Ronald Colman (A Tale of Two Cities) plays the double role in the resilient 1937 David O. Selznick production, making palpable the heartbreak of the royal stand-in whose gallantry is tested by his love for the real king’s fiancée (Madeleine Carroll). (Warner Bros)
Cast: Director: Max Ophüls | Writer: Written by Douglas Fairbanks Jr. based on the novel "His Majesty, The King" by Cosmo Hamilton | Starring: Douglas Fairbanks Jr., Maria Montez, Rita Corday [as Paule Croset], Henry Daniell, Nigel Bruce | Year: 1947 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: John Cromwell | Writer: Screen play by John L. Balderston based on Edward Rose's dramatization of Anthony Hope's novel | Starring: Ronald Colman, Madeleine Carroll, Douglas Fairbanks Jr., Mary Astor, C. Aubrey Smith, David Niven, Raymond Massey | Year: 1937 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 101 minuti

Orari: Thu April 02 - 7:30 pm / 9:35 pm

item.title: Casablanca
item.description:
Screen icons Humphrey Bogart and Ingrid Bergman headline one of filmdom’s most enduring romances! While WWII rages across Europe, an American ex-pat in Vichy-controlled Casablanca reconnects with a smoldering former flame and must decide whether to reignite their affair or help her and her fugitive husband escape the Nazis. Director Michael Curtiz’s masterpiece continues to amaze, filled with career-defining performances from its exquisite ensemble cast, plus a timeless Max Steiner score.
Cast: Director: Michael Curtiz | Writer: screen play by Julius J. Epstein and Philip G. Epstein and Howard Koch from a play by Murray Burnett and Joan Alison | Starring: Humphrey Bogart, Ingrid Bergman, Paul Henreid, Claude Rains, Conrad Veidt, Sydney Greenstreet, Peter Lorre | Year: 1942 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 102 minuti

Orari: Sat April 04 - 2:00 pm

item.title: I Cover the Waterfront / Daniel Boone (1936)
item.description:
A newspaper reporter in San Diego is assigned to investigate the city’s seedy waterfront and uncover the truth behind rumors of human trafficking. But he soon becomes romantically entangled with the daughter of the man he’s investigating. Ben Lyon & Claudette Colbert star in a stirring pre-code romantic drama. Lyon would later become a studio exec at 20 th Century Fox, where he would screen test a young Norma Jeane Dougherty and sign her to her first studio contract, rebranding her as Marilyn Monroe. One of the most thrilling stories of American history ever filmed! The tale of famed pioneer, frontiersman and folk hero Daniel Boone explodes across the big screen in an exciting adventure featuring George O’Brien as the western hero. In 1775, Boone leads a group of families into Kentucky, where they face a series of perilous dangers and rousing escapes. John Carradine co-stars as dangerous renegade Simon Girty.
Cast: Director: James Cruze | Starring: Ben Lyon, Claudette Colbert, Ernest Torrence, Hobart Cavanaugh | Year: 1933 | Country: USA | Format: 16mm | Director: David Howard | Starring: George O'Brien, Heather Angel, John Carradine, Ralph Forbes, George Regas | Year: 1936 | Country: USA | Format: 16mm
Durata: 75 minuti

Orari: Mon April 06 - 7:30 pm / 9:10 pm

item.title: Animal Crackers
item.description:
In perhaps their most frequently quoted film, Animal Crackers , the Marx Brothers transform their smash-hit Broadway musical to the big screen with uproarious results. An aristocratic house party in honor of famed explorer Captain Spaulding (Groucho) may have met its match when the Brothers unleash their trademark brand of wordplay, musical comedy and slapstick. “Groucho shines; Harpo remains a pantomimic clown who ranks with the highest; Chico adds an unusual comedy sense to his dialog as well as business and piano playing; and Zeppo, if in on a split, is lucky.” – Variety
Cast: Director: Victor Heerman | Starring: Groucho Marx, Harpo Marx, Chico Marx, Zeppo Marx, Lillian Roth, Margaret Dumont | Year: 1930 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 98 minuti

Orari: Sun April 12 - 2:00 pm

item.title: Little Annie Rooney (Silent with Live Music Score) / Sparrows (Silent with Live Music Score)
item.description:
Featuring live musical accompaniment by Cliff Retallick. “America’s Sweetheart” Mary Pickford writes, produces, and stars in a classic of silent cinema; one of the highest grossing films of 1925! Little Annie Rooney is the wildcat daughter of a neighborhood police officer, leading her her ragtag band of ruffians rampant across the Bowery. But local gang warfare could get complicated when sparks start to fly between Annie and her rival. Featuring live musical accompaniment by Cliff Retallick. Here is life’s song of love, beating with laughs and sobs, its melody in tune with your own heartstrings! The “Queen of Movies,” Mary Pickford, reteams with her Little Annie Rooney director William Beaudine for one of the most famous films of the silent era. Pickford plays the oldest in a pack of orphans being harshly mistreated on a farm hidden deep in the bayou, who must lead them to safety and a better life. Selected by the Library of Congress in 2025 for preservation in the National Film Registry as “culturally, historically or aesthetically significant,” Sparrows remains as potent & powerful as it was when originally released.
Cast: Director: William Beaudine | Starring: Mary Pickford, William Haines, Walter James, Gordon Griffith | Year: 1925 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: William Beaudine | Starring: Mary Pickford, Roy Stewart, Mary Louise Miller, Gustav von Seyffertitz | Year: 1926 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 94 minuti

Orari: Sun April 12 - 6:30 pm / 8:35 pm

item.title: Training Day / Narc
item.description:
Denzel Washington delivers an Academy Award-winning performance opposite Ethan Hawke in this gritty drama set in the morally ambiguous world of undercover police work. Every day a war rages between drug dealers and cops on the streets of America’s inner cities. With every war come casualties, none greater than 13-year veteran Los Angeles narcotics officer Alonzo Harris (Washington), whose questionable methods blur the line between legal and corrupt. Today Alonzo gets a new partner, idealistic rookie Jake Hoyt (Hawke), and Jake has one day – and one day only – to prove his mettle to his fiercely charismatic superior. Over 24 hours, Jake will be dragged into the ethical mire of Alonzo’s logic as both men risk their careers and their lives to serve conflicting notions of justice. (Warner Bros) Jason Patric and Ray Liotta star in this sizzling action-drama that Elvis Mitchell at The New York Times says ”…has the velocity of a hot slug from the barrel of a gun.” Patric stars as Nick Tellis, a suspended narcotics officer recruited to investigate the murder of Michael Calvess, a rookie cop killed under mysterious circumstances. Tellis is teamed with Valvess’ partner, Henry Oak (Liotta), a hot-tempered renegade who’ll stop at nothing to avenge his friend’s death. As Tellis and Oak follow a shadowy trail through the sunny drug underworld, the lines start to blur – between right and wrong, good and evil, and justice and revenge. (Paramount)
Cast: Director: Antoine Fuqua | Writer: David Ayer | Starring: Denzel Washington, Ethan Hawke, Scott Glenn, Cliff Curtis, Dr. Dre, Snoop Dogg | Year: 2001 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Joe Carnahan | Writer: Joe Carnahan | Starring: Jason Patric, Ray Liotta, Busta Rhymes, Chi McBride | Year: 2002 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 105 minuti

Orari: Thu April 23 - 7:30 pm / 10:00 pm

item.title: Tenebrae
item.description:
Dario Argento’s legendary giallo in glorious 35mm! An American author in Rome is stalked by a black-gloved, razor-wielding psycho hell-bent on annihilating everyone close to the wordsmith. Shocking bursts of depraved violence, ever-prowling camera work, and a pulse-pounding score from Goblin’s Simonetti-Morante-Pignatelli topline this infamous video nasty from the sadistic imagination of Italy’s master of horror.
Cast: Director: Dario Argento | Writer: Dario Argento | Starring: Anthony Franciosa, John Saxon, Daria Nicolodi, Christian Borromeo, Mirella D'Angelo, Veronica Lario, Ania Pieroni, Carola Stagnaro, John Steiner, Lara Wendel, Giuliano Gemma | Year: 1982 | Country: Italy | Format: 35mm
Durata: 101 minuti

Orari: Sat April 11 - 11:59 pm

item.title: Reservoir Dogs
item.description:
Seven total strangers team up for the perfect crime. They don’t know each other’s name. But they’ve got each other’s color. Quentin Tarantino’s incendiary directorial debut ignited the filmmaker’s distinctive voice and united him with some of his key players. When a jewelry store robbery goes wrong, Reservoir Dogs jumps between the crime’s setup and its bloody aftermath as the men try to ferret out who might be the rat in their ranks. “A brash, brutal crime-caper film, Reservoir Dogs has enough raw energy for 10 motion pictures and more than enough rough stuff to traumatize the sensitive. But not only does Dogs have teeth, it has brains.” – Jay Boyar, Orlando Sentinel “It’s extremely well-acted, written with flair and directed by a 29-year-old first-timer, Quentin Tarantino, who always knows where to put the camera, when to cut to a flashback and how to draw the best work from his brilliant cast.” – John Hartl, Film.com
Cast: Director: Quentin Tarantino | Writer: Quentin Tarantino | Starring: Harvey Keitel, Tim Roth, Michael Madsen, Chris Penn, Steve Buscemi, Lawrence Tierney, Eddie Bunker, Quentin Tarantino, Randy Brooks, Kirk Baltz, Steven Wright | Year: 1992 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 99 minuti

Orari: Fri April 03 - 11:59 pm

item.title: Casablanca
item.description:
Screen icons Humphrey Bogart and Ingrid Bergman headline one of filmdom’s most enduring romances! While WWII rages across Europe, an American ex-pat in Vichy-controlled Casablanca reconnects with a smoldering former flame and must decide whether to reignite their affair or help her and her fugitive husband escape the Nazis. Director Michael Curtiz’s masterpiece continues to amaze, filled with career-defining performances from its exquisite ensemble cast, plus a timeless Max Steiner score.
Cast: Director: Michael Curtiz | Writer: screen play by Julius J. Epstein and Philip G. Epstein and Howard Koch from a play by Murray Burnett and Joan Alison | Starring: Humphrey Bogart, Ingrid Bergman, Paul Henreid, Claude Rains, Conrad Veidt, Sydney Greenstreet, Peter Lorre | Year: 1942 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 102 minuti

Orari: Sun April 05 - 2:00 pm

item.title: The Old Fashioned Way / Misbehaving Ladies
item.description:
W.C. Fields battles creditors, widows and obnoxious children in the comedy classic The Old Fashioned Way . Staying one step ahead of the law, The Great McGonigle (Fields) and his impoverished theatrical troupe arrive in a small town to perform The Drunkard, a Victorian temperance play. Discovering they have only sold a handful of tickets, McGonigle promises a part to the wealthy mother of an annoying toddler (Baby LeRoy) with her appearance guaranteeing a sell-out. Staving off the local sheriff who intends to collect the receipts, McGonigle gives a performance the town will never forget in an uproarious farce featuring Fields’ legendary juggling act! (Universal Studios) Reigning as a Royal Princess or disguised as a hometown seamstress, she kept wives guessing, husbands wandering, and tongues wagging! An American woman who married a European prince returns to the States and encounters an unexpected problem: people think she’s only the princess’ dressmaker. When she’s only recognized by her ex-boyfriend, they keep the charade going to have some fun, but a series of misunderstandings could cause trouble. Lila Lee and Ben Lyon star in a pre-code rarity that’s been nearly impossible to see for more than 90 years.
Cast: Director: William Beaudine | Writer: Screenplay by Garnet Weston & Jack Cunningham from a story by Charles Bogle [W.C. Fields] | Starring: W.C. Fields, Joe Morrison, Baby LeRoy, Judith Allen | Year: 1934 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: William Beaudine | Writer: Screenplay by Julien Josephson from the story 'Queen of Main Street" by Juliet Wilbur Tompkins | Starring: Lila Lee, Ben Lyon, Louise Fazenda, Lucien Littlefield | Year: 1931 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 75 minuti

Orari: Fri April 10 - 7:00 pm / 8:40 pm

item.title: Blonde Ransom / Girl on the Spot
item.description:
Never-on-home-video ultra-rarity from director William Beaudine! When Duke Randall (Donald Cook) loses a bundle playing cards, he’ll have a week to come up with the $63,000 he owes or be forced to turn over his Broadway nightclub. And when he has a meet-crash car accident with lovely Vicki Morrison (Virginia Grey), he’ll confide his predicament. To score the cash she decides to fake her own kidnapping so that her rich uncle coughs up a ransom. But things get complicated when real kidnappers enter the mix. She hid out for fun… and held out for love! Co-starring Pinky Lee, noted 1950s kids-tv personality and proto-Pee-wee Herman. A musical crime mega-rarity from Universal Pictures and director William Beaudine featuring tunes by Gilbert and Sullivan! When a hopeful singer shows up for her audition with a successful talent agent, she unwittingly steps into a murder investigation. But learning the real killer is a fan of Gilbert and Sullivan, can they launch a Broadway production to help draw him out of hiding? Lois Collier (seen in the Marx Brothers’ A Night in Casablanca the same year) plays the Girl on the Spot .
Cast: Director: William Beaudine | Writer: Screenplay by M. Coates Webster, Story by Robert T. Shannon | Starring: Donald Cook, Virginia Grey, Pinky Lee, Collette Lyons | Year: 1945 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: William Beaudine | Writer: Screenplay by Dorcas Cochran and Jerry Warner, Story by George Blake and Jack Hartfield | Starring: Lois Collier, Jess Barker, George Dolenz, Fuzzy Knight | Year: 1946 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 75 minuti

Orari: Wed April 08 - 7:30 pm / 9:10 pm

item.title: Three Wise Girls / Men in Her Life
item.description:
They scoffed at love – laughed at marriage – lived for luxury alone! A small town girl heads to the big city, where she and her gal pals run into a series of professional and romantic troubles. Top-billed for the first time, Jean Harlow stars alongside Mae Clarke, Marie Prevost, Walter Byron, and Andy Devine in an entertaining pre-code film from Columbia Pictures and director William Beaudine. A formerly wealthy socialite finds herself abandoned by her new suitor when he discovers she’s now broke. While trying to pick up the pieces of her life, she meets a gruff retired bootlegger who offers to pay her debts if she can instruct him how to be a gentleman. But complications ensue in this Pygmalion tale when her previous flame returns. Made during director William Beaudine’s brief association with Columbia Pictures, Men in Her Life is a prime slice of pre-code entertainment.
Cast: Director: William Beaudine | Starring: Jean Harlow, Mae Clarke, Walter Byron, Marie Prevost, Andy Devine | Year: 1932 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: William Beaudine | Starring: Lois Moran, Charles Bickford, Victor Varconi, Donald Dillaway | Year: 1931 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 75 minuti

Orari: Fri April 17 - 7:00 pm / 8:40 pm

item.title: Reservoir Dogs
item.description:
Seven total strangers team up for the perfect crime. They don’t know each other’s name. But they’ve got each other’s color. Quentin Tarantino’s incendiary directorial debut ignited the filmmaker’s distinctive voice and united him with some of his key players. When a jewelry store robbery goes wrong, Reservoir Dogs jumps between the crime’s setup and its bloody aftermath as the men try to ferret out who might be the rat in their ranks. “A brash, brutal crime-caper film, Reservoir Dogs has enough raw energy for 10 motion pictures and more than enough rough stuff to traumatize the sensitive. But not only does Dogs have teeth, it has brains.” – Jay Boyar, Orlando Sentinel “It’s extremely well-acted, written with flair and directed by a 29-year-old first-timer, Quentin Tarantino, who always knows where to put the camera, when to cut to a flashback and how to draw the best work from his brilliant cast.” – John Hartl, Film.com
Cast: Director: Quentin Tarantino | Writer: Quentin Tarantino | Starring: Harvey Keitel, Tim Roth, Michael Madsen, Chris Penn, Steve Buscemi, Lawrence Tierney, Eddie Bunker, Quentin Tarantino, Randy Brooks, Kirk Baltz, Steven Wright | Year: 1992 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 99 minuti

Orari: Fri April 17 - 11:59 pm

item.title: The Mystery of the 13th Guest / The Living Ghost / The Face of Marble
item.description:
An unseen terror holds an entire family in fear! On her 21 st birthday, a woman returns to her grandfather’s vacant estate thirteen years after a strange family dinner party… with murder soon following. Director William Beaudine helms a poverty row mystery packed with chills & chuckles, shadowy twists & surprising secrets over its quick-moving hour-long runtime. Frequent Beaudine collaborator Tim Ryan co-writes the script and co-stars as the police detective. James Dunn stars as Nick Trayne, a fast-talking former detective hired to look for a missing banker. But during his investigation, the missing man turns up in a mysterious zombie-like state. And when this living ghost’s brother-in-law turns up dead, could he be the killer or is someone else controlling him? Director William Beaudine crafts a charmingly comedic whodunnit quickie. The last gasp in chillers! A doctor obsessed with reviving the recently deceased unleashes a dangerous creation. Meanwhile in his isolated manor, his lonely wife has eyes for another. John Carradine stars in an eerie spookshow from Monogram Pictures and director William Beaudine.
Cast: Director: William Beaudine | Writer: Screenplay by Tim Ryan and Charles Marion and Arthur Hoerl from the novel "The 13th Guest" by Armitage Trail | Starring: Helen Parrish, Dick Purcell, Tim Ryan, Frank Faylen | Year: 1943 | Country: USA | Format: 16mm | Director: William Beaudine | Writer: Screenplay by Joseph Hoffman, Original story by Howard Dimsdale | Starring: James Dunn, Joan Woodbury, Paul McVey, Vera Gordon | Year: 1942 | Country: USA | Format: 16mm | Director: William Beaudine | Starring: John Carradine, Claudia Drake, Robert Shayne, Maris Wrixon, Willie Best | Year: 1946 | Country: USA | Format: 16mm
Durata: 72 minuti

Orari: Thu April 16 - 7:30 pm / 9:00 pm / 10:20 pm

item.title: Training Day / Narc
item.description:
Denzel Washington delivers an Academy Award-winning performance opposite Ethan Hawke in this gritty drama set in the morally ambiguous world of undercover police work. Every day a war rages between drug dealers and cops on the streets of America’s inner cities. With every war come casualties, none greater than 13-year veteran Los Angeles narcotics officer Alonzo Harris (Washington), whose questionable methods blur the line between legal and corrupt. Today Alonzo gets a new partner, idealistic rookie Jake Hoyt (Hawke), and Jake has one day – and one day only – to prove his mettle to his fiercely charismatic superior. Over 24 hours, Jake will be dragged into the ethical mire of Alonzo’s logic as both men risk their careers and their lives to serve conflicting notions of justice. (Warner Bros) Jason Patric and Ray Liotta star in this sizzling action-drama that Elvis Mitchell at The New York Times says ”…has the velocity of a hot slug from the barrel of a gun.” Patric stars as Nick Tellis, a suspended narcotics officer recruited to investigate the murder of Michael Calvess, a rookie cop killed under mysterious circumstances. Tellis is teamed with Valvess’ partner, Henry Oak (Liotta), a hot-tempered renegade who’ll stop at nothing to avenge his friend’s death. As Tellis and Oak follow a shadowy trail through the sunny drug underworld, the lines start to blur – between right and wrong, good and evil, and justice and revenge. (Paramount)
Cast: Director: Antoine Fuqua | Writer: David Ayer | Starring: Denzel Washington, Ethan Hawke, Scott Glenn, Cliff Curtis, Dr. Dre, Snoop Dogg | Year: 2001 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: Joe Carnahan | Writer: Joe Carnahan | Starring: Jason Patric, Ray Liotta, Busta Rhymes, Chi McBride | Year: 2002 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 105 minuti

Orari: Sat April 25 - 6:00 pm / 8:30 pm

item.title: Blonde Ransom / Girl on the Spot
item.description:
Never-on-home-video ultra-rarity from director William Beaudine! When Duke Randall (Donald Cook) loses a bundle playing cards, he’ll have a week to come up with the $63,000 he owes or be forced to turn over his Broadway nightclub. And when he has a meet-crash car accident with lovely Vicki Morrison (Virginia Grey), he’ll confide his predicament. To score the cash she decides to fake her own kidnapping so that her rich uncle coughs up a ransom. But things get complicated when real kidnappers enter the mix. She hid out for fun… and held out for love! Co-starring Pinky Lee, noted 1950s kids-tv personality and proto-Pee-wee Herman. A musical crime mega-rarity from Universal Pictures and director William Beaudine featuring tunes by Gilbert and Sullivan! When a hopeful singer shows up for her audition with a successful talent agent, she unwittingly steps into a murder investigation. But learning the real killer is a fan of Gilbert and Sullivan, can they launch a Broadway production to help draw him out of hiding? Lois Collier (seen in the Marx Brothers’ A Night in Casablanca the same year) plays the Girl on the Spot .
Cast: Director: William Beaudine | Writer: Screenplay by M. Coates Webster, Story by Robert T. Shannon | Starring: Donald Cook, Virginia Grey, Pinky Lee, Collette Lyons | Year: 1945 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm | Director: William Beaudine | Writer: Screenplay by Dorcas Cochran and Jerry Warner, Story by George Blake and Jack Hartfield | Starring: Lois Collier, Jess Barker, George Dolenz, Fuzzy Knight | Year: 1946 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 75 minuti

Orari: Tue April 07 - 7:30 pm / 9:10 pm

item.title: The Ape Man / Voodoo Man / Bela Lugosi Meets A Brooklyn Gorilla
item.description:
No one is safe from the cruel desires of this inhuman fiend! It’s WEIRD! It’s WILD! It’s a tale of TERROR you won’t forget! An experiment turns a scientist into a crazed half-human, half-ape killer and now he’ll have to scramble to find a cure. Bela Lugosi stars in a horror fave from producer Sam Katsman and director William Beaudine. You dare not look into his eyes! His lust for voodooism spells D-O-O-M! A madman lures women to his mansion with plans to use them to revive his dead wife. Bela Lugosi, John Carradine, and George Zucco star in a classic low budget chiller from producer Sam Katzman and director William Beaudine. Crooner (and future Massacre Mafia Style / Gone with the Pope filmmaker) Duke Mitchell and rubber-faced comedian Sam Patrello take their Martin & Lewis-style nightclub act to the big screen in a horror film that will stiffen you with laughter! When the pair land on the exotic island of Cola Cola, they’ll entertain the locals (while Duke woos the chief’s daughter) and try to find a way home. Will the mysterious scientist Dr. Zabor (Bela Lugosi) be able to help? William Beaudine directs from a script by his frequent Bowery Boys collaborator Tim Ryan.
Cast: Director: William Beaudine | Writer: Screenplay by Barney Sarecky, Original story by Karl Brown | Starring: Bela Lugosi, Louise Currie, Wallace Ford, Henry Hall | Year: 1943 | Country: USA | Format: 16mm | Director: William Beaudine | Writer: Original Story & Screenplay by Robert Charles | Starring: Bela Lugosi, John Carradine, George Zucco, Wanda McKay, Louise Currie | Year: 1944 | Country: USA | Format: 16mm | Director: William Beaudine | Writer: Screen play by Tim Ryan | Starring: Bela Lugosi, Duke Mitchell, Sammy Petrillo, Charlita, Muriel Landers, Al Kikume | Year: 1952 | Country: USA | Format: 16mm
Durata: 74 minuti

Orari: Mon April 27 - 7:30 pm / 9:05 pm / 10:25 pm

item.title: Kill Bill: The Whole Bloody Affair
item.description:
Quentin Tarantino’s Kill Bill: The Whole Bloody Affair unites Volume 1 and Volume 2 into a single unrated epic – presented exactly as he intended, complete with a new, never-before-seen anime sequence. Uma Thurman stars as The Bride, left for dead after her former boss and lover Bill ambushes her wedding rehearsal, shooting her in the head and stealing her unborn child. To exact her vengeance, she must first hunt down the four remaining members of the Deadly Viper Assassination Squad before confronting Bill himself. With its operatic scope, relentless action, and iconic style, The Whole Bloody Affair stands as one of cinema’s definitive revenge sagas – rarely shown in its complete form and now presented with a classic intermission.
Cast: Director: Quentin Tarantino | Writer: Quentin Tarantino | Starring: Uma Thurman, Lucy Liu, Vivica A. Fox, Michael Madsen, Daryl Hannah, Gordon Liu, Michael Parks, David Carradine | Year: 2025 | Country: USA | Format: 35mm
Durata: 275 minuti

Orari: Fri April 03 - 5:30 pm
